title: Boat Rental in Loch Lomond: Things to Do on the Water in 2026
html:
Loch Lomond, the largest lake in Great Britain, offers some of the finest boating in Scotland. Depart from Balloch Pier or Luss to explore its islands, with rentals from around €180 for a half-day motorboat. BoatHire24 connects you with verified owners for a smooth booking experience.

Where to set off in Loch Lomond

Several marinas and villages provide ideal departure points for your adventure. Balloch Pier sits at the southern end, near the Loch Lomond Shores complex. Luss Marina is a popular spot with parking and facilities. For a quieter launch, try Milarrochy Bay or Rowardennan Pier. The islands of Inchmurrin and Inchcailloch are reachable from most points, offering secluded anchorages. One option worth a look is the Fairline Targa 43 for hire.

Which boat to rent

Choose from a range of boats to match your group size and activity preference. BoatHire24 lists options from small day boats to luxury yachts. Here are four types suited to Loch Lomond:

  1. Motorboat (5, 6 guests), Ideal for a family day out, from €180 for a half-day.
  2. RIB (tender, 4, 8 guests), Fast and nimble for water sports or island hopping, from €250 for two hours.
  3. Sailing yacht (6, 10 guests), Relaxed cruising with wind power, from €400 for a full day.
  4. Luxury motor yacht (8, 12 guests), Spacious and well-appointed, like the Fairline Targa 43 for hire, from €850 per day.

Experienced boaters can also rent a helm-yourself vessel; check each listing's licence requirements.

How much does it cost to rent a boat in Loch Lomond?

Prices vary widely depending on boat type and duration, generally from €80 for a shared tour to €1,500 for a crewed yacht. A shared tour on a RIB costs about €80 per person for a 90-minute cruise. A small motorboat for a half-day (up to 4 guests) starts at €180. A catamaran for a full day (up to 12 guests) ranges from €600 to €1,200. A crewed luxury yacht with skipper is around €1,500 per day. BoatHire24 lets you browse boats for your dates and compare prices easily.

Do you need a licence to rent a boat in Loch Lomond?

For many small motorboats and RIBs, no licence is required, but larger yachts may need proof of competency. The operator will confirm exact requirements when you book, never assume. A short briefing covers basic safety and navigation. Licensed boaters can skip the briefing on some vessels.

Local tip: avoid the busiest months of July and August; June and early September offer calmer winds (the prevailing south-westerly is mild) and fewer crowds at Luss.

At a glance

  • Price band: €80 to €1,500 per day
  • Peak months: July and August
  • Trip length: 2 hours to full day
  • Capacity: up to 12 guests
  • Currency: EUR (prices quoted in euros)

Questions about boat rental in Loch Lomond

Can I take a boat from Loch Lomond to the sea?

No, Loch Lomond is a freshwater lake with no navigable connection to the sea; all boating is within the loch.

Are there any speed limits on Loch Lomond?

Yes, a 6-knot speed limit applies within 100 metres of the shore, increasing to 9 knots in some narrow channels; open water has no limit.

What should I bring for a day on the loch?

Bring sunscreen, waterproofs, a picnic, and a lifejacket (provided with the boat), the weather can change quickly.

Can I book a boat with a skipper?

Yes, many listings include a skipper; you can filter on BoatHire24 to find crewed options.

Practicalities: how booking works

BoatHire24 handles everything through its online platform. You search by date, boat type, and location, then book directly with the owner. For detailed answers, refer to the booking FAQ.
